name: TAO Compression Fakeout Reversion v1
thesis: TAO 4h is the best structurally different lane for testing a compression fakeout that resolves back into balance rather than into continuation. The edge should come from role reassignment: compression is only a setup, while reacceptance provides direction.
invalidation_condition: Abort if compression setups are too rare, if the fakeout and reacceptance logic conflict, or if reversions do not travel far enough toward balance before time decay.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A compressed market probes one side of balance, fails to continue, and rotates back after value is reaccepted.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In choppy conditions, compression often leads to failed directional probes rather than trend continuation; TAO gives a distinct volatility personality to test that structural idea.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is appropriate because it is the authorized structurally different lane and should stress-test whether the role reassignment works beyond ETH/BTC behavior.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If TAO resolves compression by genuine expansion instead of failed probes, the reversion logic will be on the wrong side of the move.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If setups appear but reversions are too shallow, reduce target ambition before narrowing the compression threshold.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: roles are deliberately separated; compression is setup only and EMA close provides direction
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: kept manageable by using moderate compression thresholds instead of extreme squeeze-only conditions

long: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.25 * ATR_14
short: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.25 * ATR_14
